title: Reading and Use of English - Part 8
description: Read the four texts about learning design. For questions 47 – 56, choose from the students (A – D).

texts.A:
### The Traditionalist (Leo)
Leo is studying at an elite design academy. [48]He admits he was frustrated at first by the requirement to spend a whole year drawing by hand before touching a computer.[/48] [52]However, he now realizes that this built a 'muscle memory' and a spatial awareness that digital tools can't provide.[/52] [55]He mentions that the high tuition fees at his school create a high-pressure environment where students are intensely competitive.[/55] [51]For him, the value of the school is the prestige of the degree and the connections to major industry players.[/51]
texts.B:
### The Self-Taught (Mara)
Mara learned everything through YouTube and online forums. [47]'I didn't have to follow a rigid curriculum, so I could focus immediately on what I was passionate about,' she says.[/47] [53]She admits that her knowledge has 'gaps', especially in the history and theory of design, which she ignored in favor of technical skills.[/53] [50]She mentions that building a portfolio was more important than any certificate for getting her first job.[/50] [56]She points out that she saved thousands of dollars by avoiding formal education.[/56]
texts.C:
### The Career-Switcher (Sam)
Sam left a career in law to study UX (User Experience) design. [49]He mentions that his analytical skills from his law background were surprisingly useful for mapping out user journeys.[/49] [54]He highlights the difficulty of being the oldest person in his class, surrounded by 'digital natives' for whom the tech was second nature.[/54] [52]He found that the most important lesson was 'empathy'—learning to see the product through the eyes of the most frustrated user.[/52] For Sam, design is a form of problem-solving rather than just aesthetics.
texts.D:
### The Apprentice (Nina)
Nina is doing a work-study program at a sustainable furniture company. [47]She appreciates that she can see the immediate real-world application of what she learns in the classroom.[/47] [53]She admits that balancing a full-time job with night classes is physically and mentally draining.[/53] [50]She mentions that working with physical materials like wood and metal taught her about the 'honesty' of materials—some things just don't work in the real world, no matter how good they look on a screen.[/50] [51]For her, the mentor-student relationship is the most valuable part of her education.[/51]

questions.question: mention the benefit of a flexible or non-traditional learning path?
questions.explanation: Mara (B) mentions not having a rigid curriculum; Nina (D) mentions the work-study balance.

questions.question: describe an initial annoyance with a specific educational requirement?
questions.explanation: Leo was frustrated by having to draw by hand for a year before using computers.

questions.question: note how experience from a completely different field was beneficial?
questions.explanation: Sam mentions his law background was useful for mapping user journeys.

questions.question: mention that working with real objects taught them limitations that digital tools don't show?
questions.explanation: Nina mentions that physical materials taught her what doesn't work in the real world.

questions.question: value the personal or professional network provided by their path?
questions.explanation: Leo (A) values industry connections; Nina (D) values her mentor.

questions.question: mention a specific psychological or physical skill that was developed through their training?
admit to a lack of balance or specific missing areas in their knowledge?
questions.explanation: Mara (B) admits to 'gaps' in theory; Nina (D) struggles with work-life balance.

questions.question: mention feeling out of place due to an age gap with their peers?
questions.explanation: Sam mentions being the oldest person and being surrounded by 'digital natives'.

questions.question: complain about the negative atmosphere created by the cost of their studies?
questions.explanation: Leo mentions that high fees create an 'intensely competitive' environment.

questions.question: mention the financial advantage of their chosen route?
questions.explanation: Mara mentions saving thousands of dollars by avoiding formal education.
